text: **Root cause of the missing colors:** in `src/tmux/launch.rs`, `open_agent_session()` ran `tmux new-session -d ... -- claude` *first*, then `make_session_transparent()` ran `tmux set-option -t <session> default-terminal tmux-256color` *afterward*. But tmux bakes `$TERM` into a pane's child process at fork time — a `set-option` after the process already exists never reaches it. I verified this mechanically (spawned a real tmux pane, confirmed `$TERM` stayed frozen at the pre-existing global default even after the session-level override ran) before touching any code. Fixed by setting `default-terminal`/`terminal-overrides` globally (`-g`) *before* `new-session` spawns the harness. Also fixed the malformed `terminal-overrides` value (`,*:Tc,RGB,extkeys` → `*:Tc:RGB:extkeys` — the old one had orphaned capability tokens with no term-pattern prefix). [...] Assistant: Done. PR #222 merged into `master` (`cf174895`), branch and worktree cleaned up. The `tenex-edge launch` color-loss fix is live — `default-terminal`/`terminal-overrides` are now set globally before the harness spawns, so `$TERM` is correct from the first frame instead of being frozen at tmux's plain built-in default. [...] Assistant: Confirmed — the fixed `tenex-edge launch` window now renders identically to the direct baseline (orange logo, orange banner, yellow warning) and the status line shows `claude@isolated-test-mac project project [idle]` instead of the red `@te_session not set` error. Let me clean up the full-screen captures that inadvertently included your other windows. [...] **Validation:** built the fix, ran it through an isolated daemon + local NIP-29 relay (`croissant`), and had a Haiku agent drive real Terminal.app screenshots via `screencapture`. I reviewed the images myself: the fixed `tenex-edge launch claude` window renders identically to a direct `claude` launch (colored robot logo, orange banner, yellow warning) and its status line reads `claude@isolated-test-mac project project [idle]` instead of the red error. `cargo fmt`, `clippy`, and all 382 lib tests are clean.
